target: eval/dot_256x256x256_f64 (tensor_contraction::matmul_2d, the rank-2 f64 dot kernel)
outcome: REJECTED - both micro-architectural GEMM levers below the 2x bar; reverted to the scalar i-k-j kernel. Kept a new large-size bit-identity regression test.

levers_tried.lever: NB×KB cache-blocking (NB=128,KB=128), jj/kk outer loops, bit-exact ascending-l
levers_tried.result: REGRESSION ~0.60x worker-corrected. Full-width contiguous inner loop already prefetcher-friendly; B (512KB) served from L2; narrow 128-wide panels + C re-streaming across 2 k-blocks added overhead with no realized B-reuse.

levers_tried.lever: 4-row M-axis register-tiling (each B element reused for 4 outputs in registers), bit-exact ascending-l, scalar tail
levers_tried.result: ~1.09x worker-corrected (within calib-variance noise; calib swung 8.4->12.6us across runs). Kernel is near compute/L2 bound at 256^3, not B-bandwidth bound, so 4x B-reuse barely helps.

diagnosis: At 256^3 on the RCH Contabo workers the i-k-j kernel is already near single-thread compute / L2 bound. Micro-architectural reuse tricks on the SAME serial kernel cannot reach 2x. Per NO-CEILING this is the signal to change axis, not keep tuning block sizes.
next_lever: Threaded per-row GEMM (rayon par over output rows; bit-exact since each output row is summed ascending-l by a single thread) targeting ~Ncore x; OR an explicit std::simd f64xN MR×NR microkernel. Needs a dep/owner decision on threading in eval; file as a fresh bead.
bit_exactness_kept: matmul_2d remains scalar i-k-j (ascending-l), bit-identical to i-j-k. New test matmul_2d_large_bit_identical_to_ijk (m=130,k=300,n=290) guards future reworks.
validation: fj-lax matmul tests 8/8 pass; clippy -D warnings clean.
